page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 96 dedicated physical cores using the arm64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, each of the 96 vCPUs maps directly to a dedicated physical core, eliminating resource contention from simultaneous multithreading.

The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This configuration does not include local storage, meaning all storage operations rely on network-attached Elastic Block Store volumes. To support high-throughput storage and data transfer, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps. There are no onboard GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this bare-metal configuration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the arm64 architecture and bare-metal deployment model offer high compute density and direct hardware access without virtualization overhead. This makes the instance suitable for network-intensive and compute-bound workloads, such as high-performance computing, network appliances, distributed analytics, and large-scale batch processing where high baseline network bandwidth and dedicated CPU resources are required.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server utilizing the arm64 architecture. Powered by AWS Graviton processors, the instance features 96 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage. It delivers a high baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, making it highly optimized for network-heavy operations. This bare-metal configuration is designed for compute-intensive and network-bound workloads, such as high-performance computing, batch processing, and distributed analytics, offering high resource density and direct hardware access without virtualization overhead.
